---
title: "Home"
site: workflowr::wflow_site
output:
  workflowr::wflow_html:
    toc: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

This site develops **SuSiE-relax**, a fine-mapping method that lets the active
LD column relax around a reference LD matrix via an inverse-Wishart prior,
learning the relaxation level $\nu_0$. The analyses below follow the
progression of the method:

1. [SER-relax-prototype Demonstration](SER-relax-prototype-demonstrate.html) - A prototype derived by making one approximation from susie-love
 
2. [SER-relax Demonstration](SER-relax-demonstrate.html) - Making two more simple and benign approximations, we have a SER algorithm that is much faster and can still recover the true $\nu_0$ and is interpretable.

3. [SuSiE-relax Demonstration](susie-relax-demonstrate.html) - SuSiE extension of the SER model above.

4. [SuSiE-relax-prime Demonstration](susie-relax-prime-demonstrate.html) - The same SuSiE demonstration using the prime latent contribution model.

6. [Choosing lambda (low-mismatch case)](choose-lambda-low-mismatch.html) - How the ridge parameter $\lambda$ in $\bar R = (1-\lambda) R_0 + \lambda I$ affects the fine-mapping result, using the final ELBO to select $\lambda$.

7. [Choosing lambda (high-mismatch case)](choose-lambda-high-mismatch.html) - The same $\lambda$ investigation in the high-mismatch regime, where the reference panel is small and susieR on $R_0$ overfits.

8. [Choosing lambda (different-ancestry case)](choose-lambda-diff-ancestry.html) - The same $\lambda$ investigation when the GWAS and reference panels are simulated from different ancestries.

9. [Scale of q as lambda varies](q-vs-lambda.html) - How $q(\lambda)=x^\top \bar R(\lambda)^{-1}x$ changes with $\lambda$ in low-mismatch, small-reference high-mismatch, and different-ancestry settings.
